commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a source-tagged error callback to `awaitPendingManagerWork`, logs close-time pending sync/provider-init failures, and adds a helper-level regression test.

PR surface: Source +13, Tests +18. Total +31 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main still catches both rejected pending close-work promises with empty catches, and `close()` uses that helper. I did not run a live SQLite lock, disk-full, or provider outage shutdown scenario.

target.title: fix(memory-core): report pending close work errors
target.body:
## Summary

- report rejected close-time pending memory sync/provider initialization promises instead of silently discarding them
- keep close behavior non-throwing so shutdown ordering and provider cleanup behavior stay unchanged
- add regression coverage for both rejected pending close-work paths

Fixes #96766

AI-assisted: yes

## Verification

- `PATH=/Users/imprvhealth/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.1/bin:$PATH node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-memory.config.ts --configLoader runner extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.async-search.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/index.test.ts`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-async-state.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.async-search.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.ts`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: memory-core close-time pending sync/provider-init promise rejections were swallowed by empty catches, leaving shutdown-time memory sync/provider failures invisible.

Real environment tested: local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS, Node v22.22.1, branch `ben/issue-96766-pending-work-errors` rebased onto upstream `main` at `2e6e17f7c50`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `PATH=/Users/imprvhealth/.nvm/versions/node/v22.22.1/bin:$PATH node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-memory.config.ts --configLoader runner extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.async-search.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/index.test.ts`.

Evidence after fix: the targeted memory-core test run completed with `Test Files 2 passed (2)` and `Tests 58 passed (58)`. Formatting check reported `All matched files use the correct format.` and `git diff --check` exited cleanly.

Observed result after fix: rejected pending sync and provider-init promises now invoke the diagnostic callback with `pending-sync` or `pending-provider-init`, while `awaitPendingManagerWork` still resolves so close remains best-effort/non-throwing for these pending-work failures.

What was not tested: no live SQLite lock, disk-full, or provider outage shutdown was forced; the regression is covered with rejected promises at the close helper boundary and the adjacent memory manager close path remains covered by the existing `index.test.ts` surface.

## Security note

No auth, secrets, trust-boundary, upload, payment, or external-network behavior changed. The patch only reports already-thrown internal close-time errors through the existing memory logger path.

Additional local review command note: the local review command was attempted but did not start because this machine's config parser rejected `service_tier = default` before analyzing the diff.
### Scripted close-path runtime proof (2026-06-25)

Additional proof added after review request: a one-off local script imported the PR branch `MemoryIndexManager` implementation from `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.ts`, injected rejected `providerInitPromise` and `syncing` promises, then called the real `manager.close()` method. Runtime was an engine-compatible local Node v24.14.0 with `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` pointed at a disposable `/tmp` directory.

Redacted transcript:

```text
$ OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-pr-96787-proof/state node --import tsx <scripted-close-proof>
[memory] provider init failed during close: Error: scripted provider init rejection for PR 96787 proof
[memory] sync failed during close: Error: scripted pending sync rejection for PR 96787 proof
proof: database close reached after pending-work warnings
proof: manager.close resolved without throwing
```

Structured memory log records from the same run contained `WARN` entries for subsystem `memory` with these messages:

```text
memory provider init failed during close: Error: scripted provider init rejection for PR 96787 proof
memory sync failed during close: Error: scripted pending sync rejection for PR 96787 proof
```

This verifies the changed close path reports both rejected pending provider-init and pending sync work through the memory logger while preserving best-effort, non-throwing close behavior.

commands.review_summary:
The PR changes `loadWebMediaInternal` so local outbound media filenames are derived with `extractOriginalFilename` instead of the raw basename.

PR surface: Source +1. Total +1 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection gives a high-confidence path: media-store paths can be staged as `name---uuid.ext`, current main forwards the local basename as `fileName`, and Telegram passes that name into `InputFile`; I did not run a live Telegram delivery repro.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blockers are a compatibility-scoping finding and contributor or maintainer real transport proof, so this should stay in human PR review rather than an automated repair lane.
